chapter: Chapter: The Case of the Fifth Word

questions.question: Why was the boy called 'Encyclopedia'?

options.B: He remembered many facts from books

questions.explanation: He was called Encyclopedia because he read many books and remembered facts easily.

questions.question: What does Chief Brown usually do when he cannot solve a case?
questions.explanation: Chief Brown discusses difficult cases with Encyclopedia Brown during dinner.

questions.question: Why did Nolan and Davenport become friends?
questions.explanation: They met in prison and developed a friendship.

questions.question: Why did Chief Brown suspect Nolan and Davenport?

options.A: They were seen near the jewellery shop

questions.explanation: Their criminal background made Chief Brown suspicious of them.

questions.question: What was Nolan’s occupation after leaving prison?
questions.explanation: Nolan ran a small palm-tree nursery.

questions.question: Why did the police search Nolan’s house?
questions.explanation: The police searched Nolan’s house hoping to find the stolen jewellery.

questions.question: Why did Nolan write the coded message?

options.B: To secretly inform Davenport about the jewellery

questions.explanation: Nolan intended the message for Davenport to locate the hidden jewellery.

questions.question: Why did the coded message appear meaningless at first?

options.A: It was written in another language
options.B: The words were random and unusual

questions.explanation: The words looked random until the pattern was discovered.

questions.question: What helped Encyclopedia Brown solve the coded message?

options.B: Recognizing a pattern related to days

questions.explanation: He recognized that the words represented days of the week.

questions.question: Why was the word 'Fir' important in the story?

options.B: It indicated the hiding place of the jewellery

questions.explanation: The word 'Fir' pointed to the tree where the jewellery was hidden.

questions.question: What does the coded message demonstrate?
questions.explanation: The message uses a hidden pattern involving days of the week.

questions.question: What quality made Encyclopedia Brown different from other boys?
questions.explanation: His intelligence and curiosity helped him solve mysteries.

questions.question: Why did Chief Brown not tell others about his son’s abilities?

options.B: He wanted to keep it secret

questions.explanation: People might not believe that a young boy could solve complex cases.

questions.question: What does the story mainly highlight?
questions.explanation: Logical reasoning and observation help solve the mystery.

questions.question: Why was the coded message written on a calendar sheet?

options.A: It was easy to write on
options.B: It hinted at the days of the week
options.C: It was the only paper available

questions.explanation: The calendar sheet hinted that the code related to days of the week.

questions.question: What skill did Encyclopedia Brown use to understand the message?
questions.explanation: His memory and logical thinking helped him decode the message.

questions.question: Why are clues important in mystery stories?
questions.explanation: Clues guide the detective toward the solution.

questions.question: What does the story teach about problem solving?
questions.explanation: Logical thinking and observation help solve problems.

questions.question: Why did Nolan hide the jewellery?

options.C: To give it to the police

questions.explanation: Nolan and Davenport likely hid the jewellery until the situation cooled down.

questions.question: Which character solved the mystery?
questions.explanation: Encyclopedia Brown decoded the message and solved the mystery.

questions.question: What is the main conflict of the story?
questions.explanation: The story revolves around the hidden jewellery and the coded message.

questions.question: Which element makes the story interesting?
questions.explanation: The coded message creates suspense and drives the mystery.

questions.question: What does the fir tree symbolize in the story?
questions.explanation: The fir tree becomes the final clue revealing the hidden jewellery.

questions.question: Why is the story considered a detective story?

options.B: It involves solving a mystery using clues

questions.explanation: Detective stories involve solving mysteries using clues and reasoning.

questions.question: What is the main message of the story?
questions.explanation: The story highlights the importance of logic and observation in solving problems.
